evidence: acquired Blue Eye, a provider of AI-powered enterprise remote video monitoring ('RVM') services for $79 million
acquired Theatro, a maker of AI and voice-powered communication and digital workflow software for frontline workers for $174 million
acquired Silvus Technologies Holdings Inc. ... $4.4 billion and share-based compensation of $20 million August 6, 2025

AI-powered analytics can highlight a person at a facility out of hours (unusual activity), locate a missing child at a theme park (appearance search), automate video verification workflows for building access

Assist, our mission-critical AI, operationalizes intelligence across the command center... enables 911 transcription, live translation and narrative development to accelerate response and enhance reporting accuracy
SVX body-worn assistant exemplifies this strategy by converging secure voice, video and AI into a single device to offer a highly differentiated solution

AI Act places significant restrictions on the use of AI for real-time biometric identification by law enforcement, and implements significant compliance requirements on the development and use of AI for biometric identification
if we use AI that is based on data, algorithms, or other inputs that are flawed or insufficient... our business, financial condition, or results of operations may be adversely affected
we work to responsibly meet our customers needs... including through AI governance programs and internal technology oversight committees

capex_mentions.quote: acquired Blue Eye, a provider of AI-powered enterprise remote video monitoring ('RVM') services for $79 million
capex_mentions.context: November 2025 acquisition of AI-powered RVM provider

capex_mentions.quote: acquired Theatro, a maker of AI and voice-powered communication and digital workflow software for frontline workers for $174 million
capex_mentions.context: March 2025 acquisition of AI voice/workflow software company

capex_mentions.quote: Silvus Technologies Holdings Inc. ... $4.4 billion and share-based compensation of $20 million
capex_mentions.context: August 2025 acquisition; includes MANET technology with AI-powered capabilities across MCN segment

limitations: Silvus acquisition ($4.4B) is primarily a MANET/communications technology deal; AI attribution is partial as AI is one component of the broader MCN portfolio, not the sole stated rationale.
No quantified AI R&D spend or dedicated AI capex line items disclosed; investment intensity scored on acquisition prices for AI-named targets.
Narrative concreteness scored 4 (not 5) because no quantified business outcomes (%, $, time saved) are provided for deployed AI use cases — descriptions are operational but not metric-backed.

Risk disclosure includes named governance mechanisms (AI governance programs, internal technology oversight committees) but no quantified risk exposure or formal named oversight body with charter details, limiting score to 4.
